docs.title: KeyShift
docs.text: KeyShift is a self-hosted Flask web application for managing Wi-Fi SSIDs and automatically rotating passwords on UniFi and TP-Link Omada network controllers. It generates scannable QR codes for each SSID so guests can connect without ever typing a password.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description 🔑 Password Rotation Automatically rotate Wi-Fi passwords on a configurable cron schedule 📱 QR Code Generation Generate and display Wi-Fi QR codes for easy guest access 🌐 Dual Controller Support Works with both UniFi and TP-Link Omada controllers 👤 User Management Admin and standard user roles with bcrypt-hashed passwords 🐳 Docker Ready Ships with a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml 🔒 Rate Limiting & CSRF Built-in login rate limiting and CSRF protection

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • Docker Deployment
  • Configuration

docs.title: Admin Routes
docs.text:
Blueprint: admin — registered at /

All routes require @login_required unless noted.

docs.title: GET/POST /admin
docs.text:
Main admin dashboard. Accepts a ?tab= query parameter to open a specific tab (default: home).

Renders pages/admin.html with all users, all SSIDs, current app config, and the active cron schedule for the guest SSID.

docs.title: POST /log
docs.text: Accepts a JSON body { "message": "..." } and writes it to the application log under the current user's name. Used by the frontend for client-side audit logging.

docs.title: POST /updateConfig
docs.text:
Updates the controller configuration (API type, credentials, controller IP) and writes back to config.json.

Form fields: api_type, apiKey (UniFi) or username/password (Omada), controller_host, tab

docs.title: POST /manualCron/<id>
docs.text:
Sets a custom cron schedule for the SSID with the given id.

Form fields: schedule (cron expression, e.g. 0 3 * * 1)

docs.title: POST /users/add
docs.text:
Creates a new application user. Accessible during init_mode (first run) or when already authenticated.

Form fields: newUsername, newPassword, newPasswordConfirm, newRole (admin | user)

docs.title: GET /makeguest/<id>
docs.text: Designates the SSID with the given id as the active guest network (only one allowed at a time).

docs.title: GET /rescanSSIDs
docs.text: Deletes all locally stored SSIDs and re-imports them from the controller. Useful after adding or removing SSIDs on the controller.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
app/
├── __init__.py          # App factory (create_app), extension init, DB bootstrap
├── models.py            # SQLAlchemy models: user, SSID
├── pages.py             # Static page blueprint
├── cron.py              # Standalone cron script for scheduled password rotation
├── ssid.py              # Per-SSID cron entry point
├── logging_config.py    # Centralised logging configuration
├── wsgi.py              # WSGI entry point
│
├── api/
│   ├── unifi.py         # UniFi controller API client
│   └── omada.py         # TP-Link Omada controller API client
│
├── config/
│   ├── config.py        # JSON config read/write helpers
│   ├── crontab.py       # Cron job CRUD via python-crontab
│   └── file/
│       └── config.json  # Runtime config (controller IP, API credentials, Wi-Fi info)
│
├── routes/
│   ├── admin.py         # Admin dashboard, user management, SSID rescan
│   ├── auth.py          # Login / logout
│   ├── setup.py         # First-run initialisation wizard
│   └── ssid.py          # Password change, QR code, cron scheduling
│
├── utilities/
│   ├── genPW.py         # Cryptographically secure password generator
│   └── genQR.py         # Wi-Fi QR code image generator
│
├── static/              # CSS, JS, images (Bootstrap + custom)
└── templates/           # Jinja2 HTML templates

docs.title: Request Lifecycle
docs.text:
Browser → Gunicorn → Flask app (create_app)
                          ↓
                    Blueprint routing
                    ├── /login         → auth.bp
                    ├── /admin         → admin.bp
                    ├── /changepw/:id  → ssid.bp
                    ├── /initApp       → setup.bp
                    └── /             → pages.bp
                          ↓
                    SQLite (KeyShift.db via SQLAlchemy)
                    JSON config (app/config/file/config.json)
                    Controller API (UniFi / Omada)

docs.title: Extension Stack
docs.text: Extension Purpose Flask-SQLAlchemy ORM & SQLite database Flask-Migrate Database schema migrations (Alembic) Flask-Login Session-based authentication Flask-Limiter Rate limiting (200/day, 20/hour default) Flask-WTF CSRF protection Flask-Bcrypt Password hashing

docs.title: Authentication Routes
docs.text: Blueprint: auth — registered at /

docs.title: GET/POST /login
docs.text:
Renders the login page and processes login form submissions.

  • Rate limited to 5 requests per minute per IP via Flask-Limiter
  • If no users exist in the database, redirects to /initApp
  • On successful login, redirects to /admin
  • On failure, flashes a generic "Incorrect Username or Password" message (intentionally avoids revealing whether the username exists)

Form fields: username, password

docs.title: GET /logout
docs.text:
Logs out the current user and redirects to /login.

Requires: @login_required

docs.title: Cron Scheduler
docs.text:
Module: app/config/crontab.py

KeyShift manages password-rotation schedules by writing directly to a cron file at /etc/cron.d/qrCron using the python-crontab library. Each cron job is tagged with the SSID name as a comment for identification.

docs.title: createCron(ssid) → str
docs.text:
Creates a new cron job for the given SSID.

Cron command: root cd /app/app && /usr/bin/python3 -m app.ssid {ssid.id}

The schedule is set from ssid.rotateFrequency (a cron expression string).

docs.title: cronChange(ssid) → str
docs.text: Updates the schedule of an existing cron job. Finds the job by comment (SSID name) and calls job.setall(ssid.rotateFrequency).

docs.title: deleteCron(ssid) → str
docs.text: Removes the cron job for the given SSID from the cron file.

docs.title: getCrontab(comment=None) → CronJob | str
docs.text: Finds and returns the first cron job matching the given comment (SSID name). Returns an error string if not found.

docs.title: manualCron(time, name) → str
docs.text: Updates the cron schedule for a job identified by name to the given time expression.

docs.title: Cron Expression Examples
docs.text: Expression Meaning 0 3 * * * Every day at 3:00 AM 0 3 * * 1 Every Monday at 3:00 AM 0 */6 * * * Every 6 hours @daily Once per day at midnight

docs.title: Docker Deployment
docs.text: The recommended way to run KeyShift is with Docker Compose.

docs.title: 1. Download and Extract KeyShift
docs.text:
Download the latest keyshift.tar.xz release and unzip it into a directory of your choice:

# Download the release zip
wget https://github.com/helloWorld44-89/KeyShift/raw/refs/heads/main/keyshift.tar.xz

# Unzip into a 'keyshift' directory
mkdir keyshift && tar -xf keyshift.tar.xz -C keyshift

# Move into the project directory
cd keyshift

Note: If you don't have wget, you can use curl -L -o keyshift.tar.xz https://github.com/helloWorld44-89/KeyShift/raw/refs/heads/main/keyshift.tar.xz instead, or download it manually from the releases page.

docs.title: 2. Configure Environment Variables
docs.text:
cp env.example .env

Open .env in a text editor and set your values:

# Minimum required — generate a secure key with:
# python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
SECRET_KEY=your-secure-secret-key-here

FLASK_ENV=production
FLASK_DEBUG=false
TZ=America/Chicago

docs.title: 3. Start the Container
docs.text:
docker compose up -d

KeyShift will be available at http://localhost:5000.

On first launch with no existing database, you will be redirected to /initApp to complete setup.

docs.title: docker-compose.yml Overview
docs.text:
services:
  web:
    container_name: keyshift
    image: ghcr.io/helloworld44-89/keyshift:latest
    ports:
      - "5000:5000"
    volumes:
      - ./data/db:/app/instance
      - ./data/cron:/etc/cron.d
      - ./data/config:/app/app/config/file
    env_file:
      - .env
Volume Purpose ./data/db SQLite database (KeyShift.db) — persists across restarts ./data/cron Cron job files for scheduled password rotation ./data/config Controller config (config.json) — API credentials and Wi-Fi info

All persistent data lives under ./data/ in your project directory, making backups straightforward.

docs.title: Persisting Data
docs.text:
KeyShift stores all runtime data in the mounted ./data/ directories. These are created automatically on first run. To back up your instance:

cp -r ./data ./data_backup

docs.title: Rebuilding After Updates
docs.text:
When a new version of KeyShift is released, stop the container and then pull before starting it back up.

docker compose down
docker compose pull 
docker compose up -d

Your data in ./data/ will be preserved.

docs.title: Viewing Logs
docs.text: docker compose logs -f

docs.title: Stopping KeyShift
docs.text: docker compose down

docs.title: Password Generator
docs.text: Module: app/utilities/genPW.py

docs.title: genPW(length=40) → str
docs.text:
Generates a cryptographically secure random password using Python's secrets module.

Character set: uppercase, lowercase, digits, and punctuation (string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation)

Complexity requirements (all must be satisfied):

  • At least one lowercase letter
  • At least one uppercase letter
  • At least one digit
  • At least one punctuation character

The function loops until all criteria are met, guaranteeing a strong password on every call.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from app.utilities.genPW import genPW

pw = genPW()        # 40-character password (default)
pw = genPW(16)      # 16-character password

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
• str — the generated password
  • str — error message prefixed with "An Error has occured: " on exception

docs.title: QR Code Generator
docs.text: Module: app/utilities/genQR.py

docs.title: genQRCode(ssid, password=None) → str
docs.text:
Generates a Wi-Fi QR code image (PNG) for the given SSID and saves it to app/static/img/<ssidName>.png.

The QR code encodes the standard Wi-Fi configuration string format:

WIFI:S:<SSID>;T:WPA;P:<password>;;

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ssid SSID The SSID model instance password str \| None Override password. Defaults to ssid.ssidPW if not provided

docs.title: QR Code Settings
docs.text: Setting Value Version 1 (auto-fit) Error correction ERROR_CORRECT_L Box size 10 px Border 4 boxes Fill / background Black on white

docs.title: Returns
docs.text:
• str — success message with the saved file path
  • str — error message on exception

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • A UniFi or TP-Link Omada controller reachable over the network
  • Docker & Docker Compose (recommended) or a Python virtual environment

docs.title: 1. Install the App
docs.text:
• Docker reference
  • Manual installation

The app will be available at http://localhost:5000. (unless you change the port in the docker compose or wsgi.py file)

docs.title: 2. First-Time Setup
docs.text:
On first launch with no users in the database, KeyShift redirects to /initApp:

  1. Enter your controller's IP address and API credentials
  2. Create the initial admin user
  3. KeyShift will scan the controller and import all SSIDs automatically

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: • Admin panel & SSID management

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • pip
  • A system cron daemon (for password rotation)

docs.title: 1. Install dependencies
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 2. Set environment variables
docs.text:
cp env.example .env
# Edit .env — at minimum set SECRET_KEY

docs.title: 3. Initialise the database
docs.text: flask db upgrade

docs.title: 4. Run the development server
docs.text: python wsgi.py

docs.title: 5. Run with Gunicorn (production)
docs.text: gunicorn -w 4 -b 0.0.0.0:5000 wsgi:flask_app

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description SECRET_KEY Required. Flask secret key for sessions and CSRF FLASK_ENV production or development FLASK_DEBUG true / false TZ Timezone (e.g. America/Chicago)

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
After first-run setup, controller settings are stored in app/config/file/config.json:

{
  "apiType": "unifi",
  "controllerIp": "192.168.1.1",
  "apiUser": {
    "apiKey": "your-unifi-api-key"
  },
  "wifiInfo": {
    "password": "current-wifi-password"
  }
}

docs.title: Database Models
docs.text: KeyShift uses SQLite via Flask-SQLAlchemy. The database file is stored at instance/KeyShift.db.

docs.title: user
docs.text:
Represents an application user who can log in to the admin panel.

Column Type Description id Integer (PK) Auto-increment primary key username Text Unique login name password Text Bcrypt-hashed password admin Boolean True = admin role (can manage users) active Boolean Account enabled/disabled apiKey Text Per-user API token (URL-safe, 32 bytes)

docs.title: user.newUser(userName, pw, role=0)
docs.text:
Creates a new user, hashes the password with bcrypt, generates an API key, and commits to the database.

user.newUser("alice", "s3cr3t", role=1)  # role=1 → admin

docs.title: SSID
docs.text:
Represents a Wi-Fi network imported from the controller.

Column Type Description id Integer (PK) Auto-increment primary key ssidName Text Human-readable SSID name ssidPW Text Current plaintext Wi-Fi password lastChanged DateTime Timestamp of last password change siteID Text Controller site ID wlanGroupID Text WLAN group ID (Omada only) ssidID Text Unique SSID ID on the controller status Boolean SSID enabled/disabled hidden Boolean Hidden SSID flag pwRotate Boolean Password rotation enabled rotateFrequency Text Cron expression for rotation schedule qrCode Boolean QR code generation enabled isGuest Boolean Marks this as the displayed guest SSID

docs.title: SSID.newSSID(...)
docs.text: Creates and persists a new SSID record.

docs.title: ssid.makeGuest()
docs.text: Designates this SSID as the guest network. Unsets any previously set guest SSID — only one may be active at a time.

docs.title: ssid.addRotation(rotateFrequency)
docs.text: Enables or disables password rotation. Pass None to disable.

docs.title: SSID.removeAllSSIDS()
docs.text: Deletes all SSID records (used before a controller rescan).

docs.title: Omada API Integration
docs.text:
Module: app/api/omada.py

KeyShift supports TP-Link Omada controllers via the Omada REST API v2. Authentication uses username/password login to obtain a session token and omadacId.

SSL

SSL verification is disabled (verify=False) for self-signed certificates.

docs.title: OMADA.login() → dict
docs.text:
Authenticates with the Omada controller and returns the session token response (contains omadacId and CSRF-Token).

Endpoint: POST /api/v2/login

docs.title: OMADA.getSites(token) → list[str]
docs.text:
Returns a list of site IDs.

Endpoint: GET /{omadacId}/api/v2/sites

docs.title: OMADA.getWlanGroups(token, siteID) → list[str]
docs.text:
Returns WLAN group IDs for each site.

Endpoint: GET /{omadacId}/api/v2/sites/{siteID}/setting/wlans

docs.title: OMADA.getSSIDs(token, siteIDs, wlans) → list[SSID]
docs.text:
Fetches SSID details (name, PSK password, site, WLAN group, SSID ID) for all sites and WLAN groups.

Endpoint: GET /{omadacId}/api/v2/sites/{siteID}/setting/wlans/{wlanID}/ssids

docs.title: OMADA.changePW(ssid, newPW) → bool | str
docs.text:
Changes the password for a single SSID:

  1. Logs in to obtain a fresh token
  2. Fetches the current SSID payload
  3. Updates pskSetting.securityKey
  4. Sends the updated payload via PATCH

Endpoint: PATCH /{omadacId}/api/v2/sites/{siteID}/setting/wlans/{wlanGroupID}/ssids/{ssidID}

docs.title: OMADA.initDBinfo() → bool
docs.text: Full SSID import: login → get sites → get WLAN groups → get SSIDs → persist to DB.

docs.title: Setup / Initialisation Routes
docs.text:
Blueprint: setup — registered at /

These routes are only accessible during the first-run setup flow (no users in the database).

docs.title: GET/POST /initApp
docs.text:
Renders the first-run setup wizard (pages/initApp.html). Sets session['init_mode'] = True to allow unauthenticated access to the user-creation and config endpoints during setup.

Returns 404 if users already exist.

docs.title: GET /init
docs.text:
Triggers the initial database population from the controller:

  1. Reads config.json to determine the API type
  2. Calls UNIFI.initDBinfo() or OMADA.initDBinfo() to import all SSIDs
  3. Generates a QR code for each imported SSID

Returns: { "message": "Success", "details": "N SSIDs Initialized" }

Requires: authenticated user or session['init_mode']

docs.title: POST /newConfig
docs.text:
Saves the initial controller configuration during the setup wizard. Only accessible when the request originates from /initApp and init_mode is active.

Form fields: api_type, controllerIp, controllerApiKey (UniFi) or controllerUsername/controllePassword (Omada)

docs.title: SSID Routes
docs.text:
Blueprint: ssid — registered at /

All routes require @login_required.

docs.title: GET /changepw/<id>
docs.text:
Changes the Wi-Fi password for the SSID with the given id.

Query Param Value Behaviour pw auto (default) Generates a new secure 40-character password pw any string Uses the provided string as the new password

On success, updates the password in both the controller and the local database, then redirects to /admin.

docs.title: GET /qr/<id>
docs.text:
Returns the QR code image (PNG) for the given SSID. The image must already exist at app/static/img/<ssidName>.png.

Returns 404 if the image file is not found.

docs.title: GET /createCron/<id>
docs.text:
Creates, updates, or deletes the password-rotation cron job for the given SSID.

Query Param Value Behaviour rotateFrequency None Deletes the existing cron job and disables rotation rotateFrequency cron expression Creates or updates the cron job

docs.title: GET /network/qr/<id>
docs.text: Renders a guest-facing page (pages/index.html) showing the QR code for the given SSID. Intended to be displayed on a screen in a lobby or common area.

docs.title: UniFi API Integration
docs.text:
Module: app/api/unifi.py

KeyShift uses the UniFi Network Integration API (v1) to read and update Wi-Fi broadcast configurations. Authentication is via an API key passed in the X-API-KEY header.

SSL

SSL verification is disabled (verify=False) to support controllers with self-signed certificates. Consider providing a CA bundle in production.

docs.title: UNIFI.getSiteIDs() → list[str]
docs.text:
Fetches all site IDs from the controller.

Endpoint: GET /proxy/network/integration/v1/sites

docs.title: UNIFI.getWifiID(siteList) → list[str]
docs.text:
Fetches all Wi-Fi broadcast IDs across the provided sites.

Endpoint: GET /proxy/network/integration/v1/sites/{siteID}/wifi/broadcasts

docs.title: UNIFI.getSSIDInfo(siteIDs, wlans) → list[SSID]
docs.text:
Fetches full SSID details (name, password, enabled status) for every combination of site and broadcast ID.

Endpoint: GET /proxy/network/integration/v1/sites/{siteID}/wifi/broadcasts/{wlanID}

docs.title: UNIFI.initDBinfo() → bool
docs.text:
Orchestrates a full SSID import:

  1. getSiteIDs()
  2. getWifiID(siteIDs)
  3. getSSIDInfo(siteIDs, wlans)
  4. Persists each new SSID to the database (skips duplicates by ssidID)

docs.title: UNIFI.changePW(ssid, newPw) → bool | str
docs.text:
Changes the password of a single SSID on the controller.

  1. Fetches the current broadcast config via GET
  2. Strips id and metadata fields
  3. Updates securityConfiguration.passphrase
  4. Sends updated config via PUT

Returns True on success, an error string on failure.
